exampleSentencesTranslated: I really like eating mooncake during the Mid-Autumn Festival.
This year, I bought mooncake to give to my family and friends.
She often makes mooncake at home with her mother every autumn.
notes_md:
**Bánh trung thu** means *mooncake*, the traditional cake eaten during the Mid-Autumn Festival in Vietnam. It’s usually round or square and filled with sweet or savory fillings.

It’s a **countable noun**. Use classifiers like **cái** (neutral) or **chiếc** (a bit more formal/literary). You’ll also hear **hộp bánh trung thu** (a box of mooncakes) when talking about gifts.

Common pairings: **nhân đậu xanh** (mung bean filling), **nhân thập cẩm** (mixed filling), **trứng muối** (salted egg yolk), **bánh nướng** (baked) and **bánh dẻo** (soft, sticky rice style).

Strong association: Tết Trung Thu (Mid-Autumn Festival) → think family, gifts, and sharing mooncakes.
notes:

It’s a countable noun. Use classifiers like cái (neutral) or chiếc (a bit more formal/literary). You’ll also hear hộp bánh trung thu (a box of mooncakes) when talking about gifts.

Common pairings: nhân đậu xanh (mung bean filling), nhân thập cẩm (mixed filling), trứng muối (salted egg yolk), bánh nướng (baked) and bánh dẻo (soft, sticky rice style).

exampleSentences: Em rất thích ăn bánh trung thu vào dịp Tết Trung Thu.
Năm nay, anh mua bánh trung thu để tặng gia đình và bạn bè.
Chị thường cùng mẹ làm bánh trung thu ở nhà mỗi mùa thu.
